title: AST Officer Involved Shooting Investigation: Bethel [AK26026271]
summary: At the request of the Bethel Police Department, the Alaska Bureau of Investigation is investigating an officer-involved shooting that occurred in Bethel on March 25, 2026, at approximately 4:05 am. A preliminary investigation indicates an adult male had been taken to the Bethel hospital earlier in the night after standing in the roadway and refusing to move onto the sidewalk. The man left the hospital before being seen and later returned to the roadway. Officers contacted the man a second time, at which point he produced a knife and refused multiple commands to drop it. The man then approached an officer while brandishing the knife, and the officer fired, striking him. The man was medevaced to an Anchorage-area hospital for treatment of serious injuries. No other injuries were reported to
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

The subject was transported to Fairbanks Correctional Center for further detention.

title: Minister’s Message: Bright and vivid reasons
summary: Sunrises and sunsets are beautiful in the winter months. I say winter because I don’t usually see them during the summer; they are too early and too late. I don’t often see the sun begin and end its journey across the sky in the summer unless I’m going fishing or coming home from a fishing trip.

title: AST MVC - Fatal: Tok [AK21053522]
summary: On 5-19-21 at approximately 0744 hours Alaska State Troopers received the report of a single vehicle rollover on Sourdough Lane near Midnight Sun Drive, in Tok. AST responded to the scene and observed a gray, 2008, Ford Ranger, in the ditch, heavily damaged, and resting on the driver’s side. Troopers discovered that the vehicle was occupied by Craig R. Burns (age 58 from Tok) and Alicia R. Thomas (age 34 from Tok). Both Burns and Thomas were obviously deceased. Yaw marks on the gravel road indicated that the vehicle was traveling eastbound on Sourdough Lane when the driver lost control. The vehicle left the roadway on the north side and rolled. Neither occupant was wearing a seat belt. Alcohol is believed to be a factor. Tok EMS assisted with extricating the bodies from the wreckage. The S

title: AST Sexual Abuse of a Minor: Kodiak [AK21051715]
summary: Update 5/21: On May 14, 2021, Alaska State Troopers in Kodiak were notified that 19-year-old Kodiak resident Vince Jay Barcelon Deliguin has sexually assaulted a minor multiple times over the course of a year. Kodiak Troopers initiated a thorough investigation and were able to determine that Deliguin had blackmailed the victim with the threat that he would release lewd photos of the victim if the victim did not perform sexual acts with him. When Deliguin made another attempt to sexually assault the victim, Troopers were able to arrest him at the location he arranged for the victim to meet him at. Deliguin was a volunteer volleyball team student manager at Kodiak High School, where he met the victim. Deliguin was arrested on three charges of Sexual Abuse of a Minor I, five charges of Entice
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

charges: three charges of Exploitation of a Minor
three charges of Coercion; he is currently remanded at Kodiak Jail with a bail of $100

title: AST Take Caribou Closed Season: King Cove [AK21048405]
summary: On 5/8/2021, Alaska Wildlife Troopers based In King Salmon responded to King Cove based on an anonymous tip regarding the illegal take of caribou. Investigation revealed that three caribou were taken on 5/5/2021 in GMU 09D within the Izembek NWR Wilderness Area. The season closed on 3/31/2021. Jonathan Severian (49), Graydon Severian (20), Jeremiah Wilson (23), and Howard Christiansen (75), all from King Cove were charged with taking caribou during a closed season and unlawful possession. Additionally, James Gould (73) was charged with unlawful possession. The caribou were seized and donated to charity with the firearms utilized in the offense seized as well. A summons was issued for all counts through the Anchorage Court.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

charges: taking caribou during a closed season

title: AST Death Investigation: Kotlik [AK21039112]
summary: On April 15, 2021, at approximately 0800 hours, Alaska State Troopers were notified that a man had been shot inside a residence in Kotlik and was deceased. Due to poor weather in the area on 4/15, Troopers from Bethel were unable to travel to Kotlik until 4/16. Based on the investigation, 49-year-old Kotlik resident Yago Pitka was inside the home of another Kotlik resident when Pitka was asked to leave; however, he refused to leave the residence. Pitka then physically attacked the resident inside his home, and a physical altercation occurred between the two individuals. The resident then produced a firearm and fired at Pitka, striking him fatally. Pitka was declared deceased at the scene. At this time, no criminal charges have been filed, and the case will be turned over to the district at
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Drugs: Ketchikan [AK21039224]
summary: Troopers were contacted in relation to a REDDI on North Tongass Highway at Eichner Avenue that was unable to maintain its lane while driving. Troopers located the vehicle on Eichner Avenue and contacted the driver, and sole occupant, Victor Sivertson (34 Y/O), who displayed signs of narcotics use and possession. A K9 sniff was performed on the vehicle and indicated positive to the presence of the odor of narcotics. A Search Warrant was obtained for the vehicle and served. At the conclusion of the service suspected methamphetamine and narcotics consumption evidence was seized. Sivertson was arrested on one count of Misconduct Involving a Controlled Substance in the 5th degree and remanded to Ketchikan Correctional Center.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Fatal Boating Accident: Sitka [AK21036844]
summary: Update 4/16: Alaska Wildlife Troopers have determined the cause of the fatal boating accident to be a catastrophic steering system failure that threw both occupants from the skiff into the water. One of the steering cables was found broken during an inspection after the skiff was taken out of the water. Based on a preliminary investigation by the medical examiner, Mr. Merculief’s death was caused by a combination of hypothermia and drowning. Original: On Friday, April 9, 2021, at approximately 1230 hours, Alaska State Troopers and Alaska Wildlife Troopers in Sitka were notified of two individuals being thrown from a 17’ skiff in the area of the Starrigavan Boat Launch near Lisianski Point. title: AST DWLR: Mile 8, Kenai Spur Highway, Kenai AK [AK21025153]
location: Mile 8, Kenai Spur Highway, Kenai AK
summary: On 3-11-2021 at 0054 hours, the Soldotna AST K-9 Team observed a white 2000 Chevrolet S-10 commit a moving violation. A traffic stop was conducted, and the driver of the vehicle was identified Derrick L. Hurd (27) YOA of Kenai. An APSIN check revealed Hurd is driving on a revoked operator’s license for the original charge of DWLR. During this contact Hurd was found to be in possession of drug paraphernalia. A K-9 application was performed on Hurds vehicle. K-9 Donna indicated for the presence of the odor of controlled substances emitting from Hurds vehicle. The vehicle was impounded for investigative purposes and is currently awaiting the application of a search warrant. Hurd was transported and remanded for DWLR to Wildwood Pretrial pending O/R release.

title: AST Search & Rescue: Nancy Lake [AK21025093]
summary: On 3/10/21 at approximately 2223 hours, the Alaska State Troopers received a report for a search and rescue. Further investigation revealed, Richard Goodman II age 49, of Eagle River became stranded while snowmaching with his minor son. They were attempting to get to a state rental cabin for the weekend. Both parties were able to self-rescue and walk out. Mat Su Fire and EMS responded and were able to reach Richard and his son and provide transportion back to the main parking lot. Richard and his son were okay and opted to return in a couple of days to recover their snowmachine and camping supplies.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Ice Rescue, Iditarod Patrol: Skwentna [AK21023656]
summary: On 3-9-2021, the Alaska Wildlife Troopers Knier and Gunderson were at the Iditarod Dogsled Race checkpoint on the Skwentna River at Skwentna and observed an eight-year-old male child fall into a lead of open water and begin to be pulled by the current under the ice. The victim's 10-year-old brother was present and struggling to keep him from being pulled under the ice. Wildlife Trooper Knier and Iditarod Volunteer Doug Ramsey, of Sundance, Wyoming, sprinted to the child and pulled him from to water. The Alaska Wildlife Troopers would like to thank Mr. Ramsey for his selfless act of courage which prevented a tragic loss of life. We would also like to remind everyone who is on or near water to exercise caution and closely monitor their children. PHOTO: Trooper Knier and Mr. Ramsey standing n
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Vehicle Pursuit: Fairbanks [AK21017364]
summary: On February 18, 2021, at approximately 2230 hours Alaska State Troopers in Fairbanks received a report that a stolen vehicle was spotted near Davis Road in Fairbanks. The vehicle had been stolen at gunpoint a few days prior by an unknown male. Troopers located the vehicle and attempted a traffic stop; the vehicle failed to yield and lead AST on a 15-mile pursuit. During the pursuit, the driver was intentionally driving head-on towards oncoming traffic and drove the wrong way on a controlled access road. After Troopers successfully utilized a PIT maneuver, the driver intentionally rammed two AST patrol vehicles, causing significant damage to both. The driver, 33-year-old Fairbanks resident Lawrence Purdue, disabled his vehicle and was taken into custody by Troopers. After receiving medical
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Vehicle Pursuit: Akiachak [AK21017122]
summary: On February 18, 2021 at approximately 1300 hours the Akiachak Police Department notified Alaska State Troopers in Bethel about an erratic stolen white pickup truck that was enroute to Bethel. Troopers located the vehicle in Bethel and attempted a traffic stop, the vehicle failed to yield and led Troopers on a 50-mile pursuit along the ice roads between Bethel and Akiachak. Just outside of Akiachak the vehicle lost control and became stuck in a snowbank. Troopers arrested the driver, 30-year-old Akiachak resident, Charlene Peter on charges of Vehicle theft in the 1st degree, Failure to stop at the direction of a peace officer, Felony misuse of a license plates, DUI, and Reckless endangerment. Additionally, her passengers identified as 49-year-old Eddie Pasitnak of Akiachak, and 30-year-old

title: AST Wildlife Violation Fail to Salvage: Anchorage / JBER [AK21000873]
summary: On 1-4-21, at approximately 0925 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers in Anchorage investigated a shot cow moose on JBER. The investigation revealed Nathaniel Theodore Goseli, age 29 of Eagle River, shot a cow moose and failed to salvage all edible meat as required for permit hunt DM421 in GMU 14C. Goseli was issued a Alaska Uniform Citation to appear in court for failure to salvage all edible meat.

title: AST SAR: Kuskokwim River [AK21000385]
summary: On 1/2/21 at approximately 0920 hours, Troopers in Bethel were informed by the Bethel Search and Rescue that they were responding to a report of two snowmachiners that had driven into a hole in the ice on the Kuskokwim River downstream of Tuluksak. The Bethel SAR reported local residents of Tuluksak witnessed the snowmachiners drive into open water at 1935 hours on 1/1/21. On 1/4/21 two snowmachines and one body have been removed from the Kuskokwim River at the spot the two were seen driving into the hole in the ice. Searchers are continuing to search for the second individual.

title: AST Homicide: Emmonak [AK21000361]
summary: At approximately 0325 hours on January 1, 2021, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a deceased male at a residence in Emmonak. Troopers from Emmonak responded and located 39-year-old Brian Agwiak of Emmonak, deceased in his residence. Investigators from the Alaska Bureau of Investigation and Forensic Scientists from the State of Alaska Crime Lab responded to the scene to investigate. Investigation revealed that 27-year-old Rudolf Waska of Emmonak, assaulted Brian Agwiak until he was unconscious and then continued to assault him further. Agwiak later died a short time after the assault. Results from the State Medical Examiner are still pending. Waska was arrested and arraigned on charges of Murder in the 2nd Degree and Assault in the 1st Degree. Waska is being held on a $100,000 cash
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

A Trooper attempted to stop a gray sedan for driving 9 MPH over the Posted Speed in a 45 MPH zone on the Parks Highway near mile 57. The driver of the sedan failed to stop at the direction of the Trooper and the Trooper pursued the vehicle southbound. title: AST Human Remains Found: Aniak [AK20063071]
summary: On 9/6/2020 at approximately 1435 hours, Aniak AST Troopers received a call from a Knik Construction Employee reporting that during excavations for the new electric lines at the Aniak Airport, what appeared to be a human jaw bone was unearthed. Aniak AST responded to the location and confirmed the remains were in fact a human. Upon further investigation, more bones were located. The state medical examiner’s office was contacted about the incident. At this time, it is believed to be possibly an old burial site. The remains were collected, photographs taken and the remains are being sent to the SME in Anchorage for examination. Investigation is ongoing.

sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

summary: On 8-3-20 at approximately 1451 hours it was reported to the Alaska State Troopers in Tok that the Stop sign and accompanying street signs on the corner of Scoby Way and W. Spruce Street have been stollen and/or damaged multiple times since May. Twice the entire sign assembly has been stollen and twice it has been ripped out of the ground and left in the woods nearby. title: AST SAR / Body Recovery: Cordova [AK20035686]
summary: On 5/28/2020 at approximately 0357 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers in Cordova received a report of a missing male in Eyak Lake in Cordova. Investigation revealed Zakary Zeigler, age 21 of Cordova was camping at Eyak Lake with several other people. Zeigler was on a kayak paddling back to shore when he fell out of the Kayak and could not get back in. A female jumped in to attempt to save Zeigler to no avail. An Alaska Wildlife Trooper from Cordova and a U.S. Forest Service Agent responded to the lake via jet boat and recovered Zeigler’s body from the lake. USCG, Cordova PD, Cordova Fire and EMS and US Forest Service were also on scene. Zeigler was not wearing a life jacket and alcohol is believed to be a factor. title: AST Taking deer closed season: Prince of Wales Island [AK20028661]
summary: On 4/27/20, Alaska Wildlife Troopers on Prince of Wales Island were notified of several deer that had been taken illegally during the closed season. AWT responded to the area and located four gut-piles, each with an unborn fawn, as well as, the heads and lower legs of four adult female deer. The deer appeared to have been recently killed (within the past 12 hours). The deer had likely been shot elsewhere and then transported to the remote log landing on Saltery Point near Hydaburg, where they were gutted and processed. Several items of evidence were located at the scene, and the investigation is ongoing. Anyone with information about this incident is encouraged to contact the Alaska Wildlife Troopers-Prince of Wales post at 907-826-2291.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Snowmachine/Musher collision: Galena [AK20015147]
summary: Update: The driver of snowmachine contacted AST and is cooperating with the ongoing investigation. Original Text: On 3/2/2020, Alaska State Troopers in Galena received a report that a musher who was participating in the Serum Run was struck the night prior, at approximately 2030 hours, by a snowmachiner about five miles outside of Galena. Investigation revealed that Kirsten Bey, 64 of Nome, was mushing to Galena from Ruby when she was struck by an unknown snowmachiner. Another participant, riding snowmachine, came upon the scene shortly after and helped transport Bey to Galena. The snowmachiner that hit the musher remained on scene until Bey was transported away. Bey was medivaced from Galena to the Fairbanks Medical Hospital with significant injuries. The incident is under investigation.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive
